skill.name: packmol
skill.description: Create, validate, and troubleshoot Packmol inputs for molecular packing, solvation, interfaces, and simulation starting structures.
skill.summary: Build Packmol input files and validate molecular packing systems for MD setup.
skill.author: Chenghao-Wu

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are false positives from markdown code fences, Packmol keywords, relative documentation links, and scientific terms. The confirmed security-relevant behavior is limited to local helper scripts that write user-selected files and optionally run the local packmol binary without a shell.

medium_findings.title: Optional Local Packmol Execution
medium_findings.description: scripts/solvate_helper.py can run the local packmol executable when the user passes the run option. The subprocess uses a fixed argv list and no shell, so command injection was not confirmed, but it still executes a local external program and should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is directly present and reachable through a user-controlled CLI flag. Risk is moderate because the command is fixed as packmol and shell execution is not used.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected File Writes
medium_findings.description: The helper scripts write generated Packmol input content to paths supplied by the user. This is expected functionality, but it can overwrite local files if invoked with an unsafe output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The writes are explicit and use CLI or API-provided filenames. This is legitimate for an input generator, but there is no overwrite confirmation or path restriction.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command findings point to markdown code fences and inline Packmol examples rather than Ruby or shell backtick execution. These examples document commands like installing Packmol or running Packmol input files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown examples, not executable source code. They do not create a command injection path inside the skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Are Not Network Code
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings are documentation resources for Packmol guides, examples, and issue trackers. No runtime network request code was found in the reviewed scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is markdown links only. The Python scripts reviewed do not import network libraries or send data externally.

low_findings.title: Relative Path References Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Path traversal findings point to relative markdown links such as references to parent directories. They do not demonstrate file reads or writes outside an intended directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documentation cross-references. No evidence was found that these links are interpreted as filesystem operations by the skill runtime.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Execution Without Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The solvation helper uses subprocess.run to invoke packmol with a fixed command list. This is safer than shell execution, but it is still external process execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact subprocess.run call is present and does not use shell=True. The remaining risk is execution of a local binary under user control of input files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Output File Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: Input generation helpers write content to local output files. This is expected for Packmol workflows but should be treated as filesystem access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are directly visible and support the skill purpose. Risk depends on the caller choosing safe output paths.

content.value_statement: Preparing valid Packmol inputs is error-prone when systems include solvents, ions, interfaces, or geometric constraints. This skill provides Packmol templates, examples, validation helpers, and troubleshooting guidance for simulation setup.
actual_capabilities: Drafts Packmol input files for boxes, mixtures, protein solvation, and liquid interfaces.
Explains Packmol parameters, constraints, file formats, and common convergence issues.
Provides Python helpers to generate inputs, validate syntax, inspect overlaps, and analyze density.
Includes example workflows for basic packing, solvation, and interface systems.
Guides users on molecule counts, ion placement, solvent shells, tolerances, and restart workflows.
limitations: Requires local Packmol and valid molecule structure files to run simulations.
Does not perform molecular dynamics, force-field assignment, or energy minimization.
Generated systems still need scientific review before production simulations.
Helper scripts operate on local files and may overwrite output paths provided by the user.

use_cases.title: Prepare Solvated Protein Inputs
use_cases.description: Generate Packmol inputs for a protein with water, ions, box padding, and validation steps before MD preparation.

use_cases.title: Build Mixed Solvent Boxes
use_cases.description: Create initial structures for solvent mixtures, additives, and pure liquids using clear molecule counts and box constraints.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Packing Failures
use_cases.description: Use troubleshooting references and validation scripts to identify syntax errors, overlaps, density problems, and convergence issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Box
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Packmol input for 1000 water molecules in a 40 angstrom cubic box. Include the required parameters and explain what files I need.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Protein Solvation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me solvate protein.pdb with a 15 angstrom water shell, neutralize a +4 charge, and add 0.15 M salt. Show the Packmol approach and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Liquid Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Packmol input for a water and chloroform interface in a rectangular periodic box. Use plane constraints and include practical molecule-count guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Failed Packing Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Packmol setup for poor convergence and overlapping atoms. Suggest changes to tolerance, constraints, molecule counts, and validation workflow.

output_examples.input: Request for a water and ethanol mixture box.
output_examples.output: A complete Packmol input plan with molecule counts, box dimensions, tolerance guidance, required PDB files, and post-run validation checks.

output_examples.input: Request to solvate a charged protein.
output: Box size and solvent shell recommendations.
Validation steps for overlaps and density.

output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering conflicting constraints, dense packing, tolerance values, restart strategies, and smaller test systems.

best_practices: Start with small molecule counts, validate the setup, then scale to the target system size.
Set a reproducible seed and record tolerance, box size, molecule counts, and structure file versions.
Always inspect output with overlap, density, and atom-count checks before downstream simulation.
anti_patterns: Do not trust a generated packed structure without scientific validation and visual inspection.
Do not use very tight boxes or conflicting constraints to force unrealistic density.
Do not run helper scripts on unreviewed paths when important files could be overwritten.

faq.question: Does this skill install Packmol?
faq.answer: No. It explains installation options, but Packmol must be installed in the local environment before execution.

faq.question: Can it generate complete molecular dynamics inputs?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on initial coordinates and Packmol setup, not force fields or MD engine configuration.

faq.question: What structure formats are covered?
faq.answer: The references cover Packmol-oriented PDB, XYZ, and Tinker file handling.

faq.question: Can it help with ions and salt concentration?
faq.answer: Yes. The solvation guidance and helper script estimate cation and anion counts from charge, concentration, and box size.

faq.question: Does it execute external commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill documentation shows commands. The solvation helper only runs Packmol when the user passes the run option.

faq.question: Is output ready for production simulation?
faq.answer: No. Users should validate geometry, density, overlaps, protonation, force-field compatibility, and equilibration settings.
